Package microui.core

Class ImageBuffer

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.ImageBuffer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
public class ImageBuffer extends SpatialView
A spatial view component for displaying and manipulating images with color tinting. ImageBuffer provides functionality for loading, displaying, and applying color tints to PImage objects, with automatic dimension clamping to prevent oversized images.

Status: Stable - Do not modify

Last Reviewed: 13.09.2025

See Also:
  • SpatialView
  • AbstractColor

  • Constructor Details

    • ImageBuffer

      public ImageBuffer()
      Constructs an ImageBuffer with default properties. The buffer is initially visible with white tint color and no loaded image.

  • Method Details

    • draw

      public void draw()
      Draws the image buffer if an image is loaded. Overrides the parent draw method to check for loaded image before rendering.
      Overrides:
      draw in class SpatialView

    • isLoaded

      public final boolean isLoaded()
      Checks if an image is currently loaded in the buffer.
      Returns:
      true if an image is loaded, false otherwise

    • set

      public final void set(processing.core.PImage image)
      Sets the image directly from a PImage object. Clamps the image dimensions to prevent oversized images.
      Parameters:
      image - the PImage to set
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if image is null

    • load

      public final void load(String path)
      Loads an image from a file path. Clamps the image dimensions to prevent oversized images.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path to load the image from
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if path is null

    • get

      public final processing.core.PImage get()
      Gets the currently loaded image.
      Returns:
      the loaded PImage, or null if no image is loaded

    • getColor

      public final AbstractColor getColor()
      Gets the current tint color applied to the image.
      Returns:
      the current tint color

    • setColor

      public final void setColor(AbstractColor color)
      Sets the tint color to apply to the image. The tint color is multiplied with the image colors during rendering.
      Parameters:
      color - the tint color to apply
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if color is null

    • removeTexture

      public final void removeTexture()
      Removes the currently loaded image texture. After calling this, isLoaded() will return false until a new image is set.
